name: U.S. States
description: State capitals, borders, nicknames, and geography

questions.q: Mount Rushmore is located in which U.S. state?

source.notes: In the Black Hills. Features 60-foot faces of Washington, Jefferson, Lincoln, Teddy Roosevelt.

questions.q: Which is the largest U.S. state by area?

source.notes: ~665,000 sq mi. Over twice the size of Texas.

questions.q: Which U.S. state is nicknamed 'The Show-Me State'?

source.notes: Origin of the nickname is disputed, but generally attributed to Congressman Willard Vandiver in 1899.

questions.q: Which small state is known as 'The Ocean State'?

source.notes: Smallest US state by area (1,214 sq mi). Over 400 miles of coastline.

questions.q: Key West, traditionally the southernmost point of the continental United States, is part of which state?

source.notes: Part of the Florida Keys. 'Southernmost Point' buoy marker is 90 miles from Cuba.

questions.q: Which U.S. state contains the southernmost point of all 50 states?

source.notes: Ka Lae (South Point) on the Big Island is the southernmost point of all 50 states.

questions.q: The Mississippi River flows from a lake in Minnesota to what body of water?

source.notes: Source: Lake Itasca, Minnesota. Length: ~2,340 miles.

questions.q: How many U.S. states begin with the letter 'P'?

source.notes: Only Pennsylvania.

questions.q: How many U.S. states begin with the letter 'M'?

source.notes: Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, Minnesota, Mississippi, Missouri, Montana.

questions.q: Which four U.S. states meet at the only point where four states share a common border?

source.notes: Known as the Four Corners. Only four-state junction in the US.

questions.q: Which state is home to Colonial Williamsburg, a major living-history museum?

source.notes: Restored 18th-century capital of the Virginia colony.

questions.q: Which U.S. state has the largest population?

source.notes: ~39 million residents. Texas is second with ~30 million.

questions.q: Denver, capital of Colorado, lies in which U.S. time zone?

source.notes: UTC−7 (standard) / UTC−6 (DST). Encompasses much of the Rocky Mountain states.

questions.q: What is the capital of Illinois?

source.notes: Chicago is the largest city but not the capital. Lincoln's hometown.

questions.q: How many states are in the contiguous United States (the 'Lower 48')?

source.notes: Excludes Alaska and Hawaii, which joined in 1959.

questions.q: Which state has the tallest state capitol building, at 34 stories and 450 feet?

source.notes: Baton Rouge, Louisiana. Built in 1932 under Governor Huey Long.

questions.q: How many states border Missouri — more than any other U.S. state (tied with Tennessee)?

source.notes: Iowa, Illinois, Kentucky, Tennessee, Arkansas, Oklahoma, Kansas, Nebraska.

questions.q: Brigham Young University (BYU) is located in Provo, which is in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Owned by The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints.

questions.q: Which is the only U.S. state whose name begins with the letter 'L'?

source.notes: Named after Louis XIV of France.

questions.q: Which of these states does NOT border a Great Lake?

source.notes: Great Lakes states: Minnesota, Wisconsin, Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Pennsylvania, New York.
